title: CARVACROL
description: Carvacrol is a monoterpenoid phenol found naturally in essential oils of oregano, thyme, and other plants. It exhibits broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity against bacteria and fungi, along with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties through modulation of immune pathways and reduction of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

research.title: Carvacrol and human health: A comprehensive review
research.summary: Carvacrol demonstrates multiple bioactivities including antimicrobial, antioxidant, and anti-inflammatory properties that make it potentially useful for clinical applications across various health conditions.

research.title: Therapeutic application of carvacrol: A comprehensive review
research.summary: Carvacrol exerts strong anti-inflammatory effects by inducing antioxidant enzymes (SOD, GPx, GR, CAT), preventing lipid peroxidation, and reducing pro-inflammatory cytokine levels while modulating LPS-induced immune responses.

research.title: Carvacrol—A Natural Phenolic Compound with Antimicrobial Properties
research.summary: Research confirms carvacrol's potent antimicrobial effects against numerous bacterial and fungal strains, demonstrating efficacy both alone and in combination with other compounds for controlling pathogens.

research.title: Carvacrol: From Ancient Flavoring to Neuromodulatory Agent
research.summary: Beyond its traditional use as a flavoring agent, carvacrol shows neuromodulatory properties and potential therapeutic applications in neurological conditions.

sentiment.summary: Limited direct user feedback available for carvacrol as a standalone supplement. Research literature is positive regarding antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, but consumer experience data is insufficient for definitive sentiment analysis.

effects.label: Antimicrobial Efficacy:
effects.description: Strong evidence for effectiveness against various bacterial and fungal strains in research settings

effects.label: Anti-inflammatory Action:
effects.description: Well-documented reduction of inflammatory markers and cytokines in experimental models

effects.label: Safety Profile:
effects.description: Generally considered safe with limited toxicity data in humans

effects.label: Synergistic Potential:
effects.description: Enhanced effectiveness when combined with other natural compounds

dosage.label: Research Dosing:
dosage.description: Studies utilize varying concentrations depending on application and target organism

dosage.label: Essential Oil Form:
dosage.description: Typically consumed as component of oregano or thyme essential oils rather than isolated compound

dosage.label: Concentration Matters:
dosage.description: Antimicrobial effects are dose-dependent with higher concentrations showing greater efficacy

dosage.label: Human Data Limited:
dosage.description: Specific dosage recommendations for supplementation not well-established in clinical literature

sideEffects.label: Toxicity Data:
sideEffects.description: Limited information on adverse effects in humans at supplemental doses

sideEffects.label: Metabolic Pathways:
sideEffects.description: Knowledge of human metabolism and degradation pathways is incomplete

sideEffects.label: Phenolic Compound Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Potential for environmental contamination and degradation products requires consideration

sideEffects.label: Safety Assessment:
sideEffects.description: More clinical data needed to establish comprehensive safety profile

availability.label: Natural Sources:
availability.description: Found in oregano, thyme, and other herb essential oils widely available commercially

availability.label: Isolated Form:
availability.description: Pure carvacrol available from specialty chemical and supplement suppliers

availability.label: Regulatory Status:
availability.description: Generally recognized as safe (GRAS) as a food flavoring agent
